# Dowse（问渠）

> Open-source local full-text file search for Windows. Dowse searches file names, PDF and Office document contents, source code, and text inside images through offline Windows OCR. It uses tantivy, jieba Chinese tokenization, a native hotkey overlay, and read-only MCP tools. Files and indexes stay on the user's computer.

## Primary pages

- [Chinese product page](https://lter.space/dowse/): Features, privacy model, comparison, installation, MCP setup, and FAQ.
- [English product page](https://lter.space/dowse/en/): English overview of local Windows file-content search, OCR, and MCP.
- [Windows file-content search guide](https://lter.space/dowse/windows-file-content-search/): How Windows Search, Everything content queries, dnGrep, and persistent full-text indexes differ.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/ltspace/dowse): Rust/Tauri/Svelte source, issues, releases, and design documents.
- [Latest Windows release](https://github.com/ltspace/dowse/releases/latest): Installer and checksums.
- [Rust API documentation](https://docs.rs/dowse): Library and CLI documentation.
- [Privacy statement](https://github.com/ltspace/dowse/blob/main/PRIVACY.md): Local storage, no telemetry, and MCP boundaries.

## Verified capabilities

- Persistent full-text index over file names and extracted content.
- Text and code files with UTF-8/GBK encoding detection.
- Text extraction from PDF, DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX.
- Offline OCR for PNG, JPG/JPEG, WebP, and BMP on Windows.
- jieba tokenization and BM25 ranking for Chinese content.
- Optional NTFS MFT enumeration and USN Journal updates; normal indexing works without administrator rights.
- Read-only MCP tools: `search`, `preview`, and `index_status`.

## Important limitations

- Image-only scanned PDFs are not currently OCRed page by page; PDF search uses the existing text layer.
- Legacy DOC, XLS, and PPT formats are not the same as supported DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX formats.
- Windows OCR and the NTFS fast path are Windows-specific; the core search library can compile elsewhere with those capabilities disabled.
